surface.name: Create and verify a first EVM API Key Wallet account with Node TypeScript
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Create and verify a first EVM API Key Wallet account with Node TypeScript. The official API Key Wallet quickstart defines a non-custodial Node route with a printed EVM address as its first observable result, before funding or transactions. Prior surface before policy commit: Unresolved at CDP custodial model, product family, authentication model, and implementation selection.

alternatives_considered.name: API Key Wallet quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a non-custodial server-owned wallet path that requires API key, Wallet Secret, runtime, EVM versus Solana, and wallet-operation choices.

alternatives_considered.name: User Wallet quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is an end-user authentication and embedded-wallet route with different actors and custody.

alternatives_considered.name: CDP CLI setup and connectivity check
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It verifies CLI authentication and wallet access but does not represent every CDP product or API.

alternatives_considered.name: Trade API quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It requires wallet model, chain, token pair, account, price or quote, and execution choices.

alternatives_considered.name: Coinbase Prime quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is an institutional API path requiring a Coinbase Prime account and portfolio.

alternatives_considered.name: Agentic Wallet CLI quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a specialized email-authenticated agent wallet with a separately claimed two-minute path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Choose the CDP product and developer outcome whose first mile should be measured, then follow its account, credential, SDK or CLI, and first-success path.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has not supplied a CDP product, custody model, runtime, network, account type, or endpoint.
The developer may not yet have a CDP account, project, API key, Wallet Secret, or verified business.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The selected EVM API Key Wallet route demonstrates first success when the terminal displays the Created EVM account label and a 0x-prefixed address.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The terminal displays the Created EVM account label and a 0x-prefixed address.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The terminal displays the Created EVM account label and a 0x-prefixed address.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The terminal displays the Created EVM account label and a 0x-prefixed address.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A CDP Portal account and project for the authenticated CDP routes.
A declared non-custodial or custodial model, product family, developer outcome, runtime or interface, and target network.
A verified business account linked to CDP for custodial API groups.
The credential set required by the selected route, such as a Secret API key, Client API key, Wallet Secret, end-user authentication, OAuth credential, or institutional key.

primary_path.action: Open the Coinbase Developer Platform API overview.
details: The page describes CDP as trusted crypto infrastructure and presents non-custodial and custodial API sets.
primary_path.input: Intent to build with Coinbase Developer Platform.
primary_path.output: CDP API model, product navigation, and shared get-started links.
primary_path.success_signal: Non-custodial APIs, custodial APIs, Get an API key, and Authentication guide are visible.

details: Non-custodial User Wallets, API Key Wallets, Onramp, and related tools are available to a CDP entity after signup.
Custodial transfers, trading orders, payment acceptance, reporting, and related groups require a verified business account linked to CDP.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected model matches who controls assets and which service is needed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The roster identifies only CDP, so the custody model is unresolved.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to a CDP Portal account and use its project.
details: CDP authentication documentation assumes an existing CDP account and says a project is included by default.
The CLI setup page says a project is auto-created on first sign-in.
primary_path.input: Eligible Coinbase identity and account details.
primary_path.output: Authenticated CDP Portal and selected project.
primary_path.success_signal: The project and API Keys area are accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The current account-creation, email verification, entity, geographic, and compliance screens are not enumerated by the developer guides.

primary_path.action: Choose the CDP product family and first result.
details: Current documentation families include wallets, payments, stablecoins, trading, onchain tools, customers, webhooks, consumer APIs, business APIs, and institutional APIs.
The choice determines whether an API key alone is sufficient or business verification, OAuth, Wallet Secret, end-user authentication, or institutional access is required.
primary_path.output: One product quickstart or API group.
primary_path.success_signal: A guide with an explicit target result is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No product family can be inferred from the company name.

primary_path.action: Choose the authentication model and configure the matching credential type.
details: Secret API keys are for server-to-server REST APIs.
Client API keys are for client-side JSON-RPC.
Non-custodial API Key Wallets additionally use a Wallet Secret.
User wallets authenticate end users, and other Coinbase product families have distinct credentials.
primary_path.input: Selected product architecture and trust boundary.
primary_path.success_signal: The credential type matches the chosen API and client location.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting the wrong key or signature algorithm produces authentication or permission errors.

primary_path.action: Choose the implementation, network, resource type, and concrete API or SDK operation.
details: The API Key Wallet quickstart alone branches by Node TypeScript versus Python, EVM versus Solana, and account creation, faucet, or transaction.
The generic authentication guide requires a request method, host, and path before it can generate and use a request-specific JWT.
primary_path.input: Runtime, chain or network, selected resource, and terminal outcome.
primary_path.output: Atomic commands, code, waits, and verification for one first-mile journey.
primary_path.success_signal: Every placeholder and branch required by the selected quickstart is resolved.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Research stops rather than imposing financial, custody, network, and runtime choices.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to a CDP Portal account, open its project, and confirm Node.js 22 or later is available locally.
details: The quickstart requires a CDP Portal account and Node 22 or later; CDP documentation says a project is included or auto-created on first sign-in.
primary_path.input: An eligible Coinbase identity and local Node environment.
primary_path.output: An authenticated CDP project and compatible runtime.
primary_path.success_signal: The project's API Keys area is accessible and Node satisfies the version requirement.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The developer guides do not enumerate current account-creation, email, entity, geographic, or compliance screens.

primary_path.action: Create a CDP API key and copy its API key ID and secret.
details: The server-side SDK uses the CDP secret API-key credentials.
primary_path.output: A CDP API key ID and secret.
primary_path.success_signal: Both key values are available for local configuration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong key type, permissions, or lost secret prevents SDK authentication.

primary_path.action: Generate and copy the Wallet Secret for the project.
details: API Key Wallet operations require this additional wallet credential.
primary_path.success_signal: The Wallet Secret is generated and stored for local use.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or mismatched Wallet Secret prevents wallet-account creation.

primary_path.action: Create the cdp-sdk-example Node project, set package type to module, and create main.ts and .env.
details: The guide uses mkdir, npm init -y, npm pkg set type=module, and empty main.ts and .env files.
primary_path.success_signal: package.json, main.ts, and .env exist in the project directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Filesystem or npm initialization errors block setup.

primary_path.action: Add CDP_API_KEY_ID, CDP_API_KEY_SECRET, and CDP_WALLET_SECRET to .env.
details: Each variable must contain the matching value created in the CDP Portal.
primary_path.output: The complete local CDP credential environment.
primary_path.success_signal: All three variables are present without placeholder values.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing, swapped, or malformed credential values cause authentication or wallet errors.

details: These packages provide the CDP client and load .env credentials.
primary_path.output: The CDP SDK and dotenv dependencies.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Dependency or supported-runtime errors prevent execution.

primary_path.action: Configure main.ts to load the environment, instantiate CdpClient, create an EVM account, and print its address.
details: Use cdp.evm.createAccount and log the resulting account address.
primary_path.input: The official Node sample and installed dependencies.
primary_path.success_signal: main.ts contains client initialization, createAccount, and output logging.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An omitted Wallet Secret or incorrect SDK configuration prevents wallet creation.

details: The SDK authenticates to CDP and creates the EVM account.
primary_path.input: The program, dependencies, credentials, and network access.
primary_path.output: A newly created EVM account or an SDK error.
primary_path.success_signal: The command prints Created EVM account followed by a 0x address.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid API credentials, Wallet Secret, project permissions, or network access prevents account creation.

primary_path.action: Verify that the printed value is the newly created EVM account address.
details: The 0x-prefixed address is the first observable wallet result; faucet funding and sending a transaction are later steps in the quickstart.
primary_path.output: A confirmed first CDP API Key Wallet account.
primary_path.success_signal: The terminal displays the Created EVM account label and a 0x-prefixed address.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No address or only an error means the candidate terminal was not reached.

branches.path: Complete CDP Initial Setup, verify and link the required business account, select the custodial API group, create credentials with the needed permissions, then follow that group's resource-specific first request.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds business verification and product eligibility before any custodial API result.

branches.condition: The selected product is non-custodial API Key Wallets with Node and EVM.
branches.path: Sign in to CDP Portal, create a CDP API key, generate a Wallet Secret, initialize a TypeScript project and .env, add CDP_API_KEY_ID, CDP_API_KEY_SECRET, and CDP_WALLET_SECRET, install @coinbase/cdp-sdk and dotenv, instantiate CdpClient, run the empty client program, add cdp.evm.createAccount, run it, and verify Created EVM account plus a 0x address. Continue only if the selected boundary includes faucet funding and a sent testnet transaction.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines the first application result as a server-controlled EVM account address and adds credential, secret, SDK, and wallet-operation work.

branches.condition: The selected product is non-custodial API Key Wallets with Python and Solana.
branches.path: Create the same portal keys, initialize and activate a Python 3.10+ virtual environment, create main.py and .env, add all three secrets, install cdp-sdk and python-dotenv, instantiate CdpClient, call cdp.solana.create_account, run the program, and verify a Solana address.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes runtime, package manager, asynchronous code, account API, and address format.

branches.condition: The developer uses the CDP CLI for a non-custodial connectivity check.
branches.path: Confirm Node 22+, install Linux keyring support if applicable, install @coinbase/cdp-cli, sign in and let CDP auto-create a project, create and download an API key file, run cdp env live --key-file, generate and download a Wallet Secret, run cdp env live --wallet-secret-file, run cdp env, then run cdp evm accounts list and confirm JSON without an auth error.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success as authenticated CLI connectivity, which may return an empty accounts list rather than creating a wallet.

branches.condition: The selected route uses a generic Secret API key and direct REST authentication.
branches.path: Choose project, Secret API Keys, and Create API key; name the key, optionally set an IP allowlist and granular permissions, select Ed25519 or ECDSA as supported, create it, copy the key ID and secret, choose a runtime, export key and request method/host/path variables, install its JWT dependency, create and run the signing script, export the two-minute JWT, send the selected endpoint with Authorization: Bearer, and inspect its API-specific response.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Requires a concrete endpoint and runtime, and its JWT must be regenerated after two minutes or for changed request details.

branches.condition: The selected product is Agentic Wallet CLI.
branches.path: Install the skill package, initiate email login, receive the six-digit OTP and flow ID, verify them, run status, and confirm Authenticated before using wallet commands.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses email OTP and a wallet server instead of CDP Portal API keys.

friction_gates.description: The developer must choose non-custodial versus custodial APIs.
Custodial API groups require a verified business account linked to CDP.
Most authenticated CDP routes require a CDP Portal account and project.
Wallets, payments, trading, onchain tools, customers, webhooks, consumer, business, and institutional APIs have different first results.
The developer must choose Secret key, Client key, Wallet Secret, user authentication, OAuth, CLI, or another product-specific model.
Secret API key material and any Wallet Secret must be copied or downloaded and stored securely.
API-key permissions and optional IP allowlists can allow or block the selected operation.
Runtime, chain, network, account type, and endpoint choices are required before atomic implementation steps exist.
Direct-request JWTs expire after two minutes, and faucet or blockchain transactions introduce confirmation waits in wallet paths.

sources.title: CDP CLI setup skill
evidence_supported: Common key, wallet, permission, and keychain failures

sources.title: Agentic Wallet CLI Quickstart
evidence_supported: Email OTP and wallet status sequence
Under-two-minute claim limited to that surface

uncertainties.question: Which CDP product and custody model should define platform first success?
uncertainties.impact: It changes eligibility, actors, credentials, financial actions, cost, and terminal verification.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Coinbase documents multiple peer product families and explicitly separates non-custodial and custodial APIs.

uncertainties.question: Which authentication model, runtime, network, and first operation should be measured?
uncertainties.impact: It changes portal fields, secrets, dependencies, code, waits, and response.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The generic entry stops at API key and authentication guidance, while each product quickstart requires additional choices.

uncertainties.question: What exact live screens create and verify a new CDP Portal account and entity?
uncertainties.impact: The common account gate cannot be counted page by page.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The guides say create or sign in to an account but do not enumerate signup, email verification, entity, region, or compliance screens.

uncertainties.question: Is the current v2 authentication page intentionally login-gated for unauthenticated readers?
uncertainties.impact: A new developer may face an unexpected documentation access step.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current overview links to /api-reference/v2/authentication, while unauthenticated retrieval redirected to a docs login page; the accessible authentication guide at /api-reference/authentication supplies the detailed workflow.

excluded_after_success: No product-specific downstream work can be excluded until custody model, product, authentication, runtime, network, and first-success boundary are selected.
